one_time_description: Williams Treaty settlement received in prior year (2019), large member distributions in current year from settlement funds
economic_notes: Old Railroad Stop (gas bar and store) operated as band enterprise. Cultural Centre project committed at $14,332,000 with $4,340,780 incurred to March 31, 2020. Subsequent to year end, First Nation incurred long-term debt of $25,000,000 at prime+0.000%. COVID-19 pandemic impacted Old Railroad Stop revenues and tent/trailer park revenues. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership distributions received.
notes: Fiscal year 2020. Large Williams Treaty settlement of $153,971,468 was received in fiscal 2019 causing prior year revenue/surplus to be extremely elevated. In 2020, member distributions of $8,868,209 from settlement funds are expensed under Administration, causing a significant deficit. Investments of $104.75M are primarily restricted settlement funds (Williams Treaty, Islands in the Trent). No long-term debt on balance sheet at March 31, 2020, but $25M debt incurred subsequent to year end for Cultural Centre. Investment income of $4,073,736 and Other revenue of $1,408,691 are listed in budget column but actual amounts not separately broken out in the revenue section — actual totals are confirmed from consolidated segment disclosure. Revenue lines taken from Statement of Operations. Schedule of Remuneration not included in financial statements provided. expenses_infrastructure mapped to Community Operations segment.
